title: Leverage gate must not throttle exposure-REDUCING orders (esp. by margin_free)
source: Adversarial code review 2026-05-29 (risk-officer + general-purpose reviewers on armed execution layer)
why: The notional leverage gate scales EVERY order including de-risking ones, and budget=min(equity_budget,margin_free). On a hedging book whose accumulated tickets shrink margin_free, a reducing order set gets throttled -> can round sub-min and skip, so the gate can PREVENT the book from getting back under leverage.
scope: risk-officer FINDING3: _apply_leverage_gate scales all orders by budget/gross when gross>budget. gross_post is also computed from unsnapped scaled qty (overstates venue reality; a scaled opener can round to zero). FIX: gate only exposure-ADDING orders; never throttle reducers by margin_free; compute post-gate gross from snapped lots. Edge-case (reconcile deltas small) but real.

history.reason: Logged from adversarial review of armed execution layer

fix(execution): adversarial review caught 2 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH bugs in armed layer
fix(execution): adversarial review caught 2 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH bugs in armed layer

_apply_leverage_gate now partitions orders: exposure-REDUCING orders pass untouched; only exposure-ADDING orders are gated (gross computed over adders only, post-gate gross too). Prevents the gate from throttling de-risking orders when margin_free is drained (hedge accumulation). B0090 12000x protection preserved (opens from flat = adders). 2 TDD tests.
fix(execution): resolve B0093/B0094/B0095 review findings; B0090 -> done
